LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMMISSION'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. $38,102.44 IN COSTS ARE AWARDED TO SNG, AS SET FORTH IN EXHIBIT 1 TO THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. SNG IS THE PREVAILING PARTY FOR PURPOSES OF THE COSTS STATUTES. THE DISPUTED COSTS ARE NOT EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED FROM BEING RECOVERED AND THUS ARE RECOVERABLE IN THE COURT'S DISCRETION PROVIDED THAT THEY ARE REASONABLE IN AMOUNT AND REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE CASE. I EXERCISE MY DISCRETION TO AWARD THE DISPUTED COSTS AND I FIND THOSE COSTS TO BE REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE CASE BECAUSE OF THE LENGTHY AND COMPLICATED RECORD, THE LARGE NUMBER OF DIFFICULT AND INVOLVED FACTUAL AND LEGAL ISSUES, AND THE HIGH DEGREE OF HELPFULNESS OF THE PARTIES' PRESENTATIONS, ESPECIALLY THE GRAPHICS, IN ENABLING ME TO GAIN A GOOD GRASP OF THE RECORD AND TO UNDERSTAND THE ISSUES AND THE PARTIES' POSITIONS ON THOSE ISSUES. THE REASONABLENESS OF THE AMOUNT OF THE COSTS IS A CLOSER CALL. ON FIRST BLUSH, THE COSTS SEEM QUITE HIGH. HOWEVER, UPON A FULL EXAMINATION OF THE PAPERS FILED ON THIS MOTION, I AM PERSUADED THAT SNG HAS MET ITS BURDEN THAT THE COSTS IT INCURRED FOR THE DISPUTED ITEMS ARE MARKET RATE AND THUS REASONABLE IN AMOUNT. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Aug-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: tom roth, esq., 415.293.7684, for plaintiff security national guaranty inc.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMMISSION's motion To Tax Costs is Granted in part. $38,102.44 in costs are awarded to SNG, as set forth in exhibit 1 to the opposition papers. SNG is the prevailing party for purposes of the costs statutes. The disputed costs are not expressly prohibited from being recovered and thus are recoverable in the court's discretion provided that they are reasonable in amount and reasonably necessary to the conduct of the case. I exercise my discretion to award the disputed costs and I find those costs to be reasonably necessary to the conduct of the case because of the lengthy and complicated record, the large number of difficult and involved factual and legal issues, and the high degree of helpfulness of the parties' presentations, especially the graphics, in enabling me to gain a good grasp of the record and to understand the issues and the parties' positions on those issues. The reasonableness of the amount of the costs is a closer call. On first blush, the costs seem quite high. However, upon a full examination of the papers filed on this motion, I am persuaded that SNG has met its burden that the costs it incurred for the disputed items are market rate and thus reasonable in amount. Counsel for plaintiff has provided proposed order to the court. Judge: HAROLD KAHN, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
